name: Housing And Education Alliance
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor serving Tampa Bay area for 20+ years, offering free homebuyer education, mortgage readiness counseling, and foreclosure prevention.
description_long:
Housing & Education Alliance (HEA) is a HUD-certified housing counseling agency based in Tampa, Florida, that has served over 57,000 families in securing homeownership for more than 20 years. The organization focuses on eliminating barriers to sustainable homeownership through education, personalized counseling, and connections to down payment assistance programs totaling up to $110,000. HEA reports helping clients secure $163 million in loans and achieve 11,500 home purchases, positioning itself as a significant player in first-time homebuyer support across central Florida.

HEA's core offerings include HUD-approved first-time homebuyer education (HomeTRACK), available both as self-paced online courses and live instructor-led classes in English and Spanish. The organization provides one-on-one housing counseling from HUD-certified bilingual counselors covering budgeting, credit improvement, mortgage readiness, and navigation of layered down payment assistance programs. Beyond purchase preparation, HEA offers post-purchase homeownership education, rental and tenant counseling, and foreclosure prevention support for households facing financial hardship. Certificates from their homebuyer education programs are accepted by lenders and DPA program administrators, with 2-year validity.

What distinguishes HEA is its comprehensive lifecycle approach to housing stability—covering pre-purchase education, purchase support, post-purchase homeowner education, and foreclosure prevention all under one organization. The organization offers free HUD-certified instructor-led classes specifically for City of Tampa and Orange County residents, reducing barriers to certification. HEA maintains a curated network of top-performing local realtors experienced with first-time homebuyer programs, and its bilingual counseling capability serves non-English speakers effectively. The breadth of post-purchase topics—including estate planning, property heir management, and scam avoidance—exceeds typical housing counselor scope.

HEA represents a legitimate nonprofit resource for housing stability, with no apparent predatory practices or hidden fees. The organization's HUD certification, long operational history, and reported impact metrics suggest credibility. However, services appear geographically concentrated in the Tampa Bay/central Florida region; national consumers seeking housing counseling should verify service availability. The website emphasizes rental support availability "based on funds availability," suggesting potential resource constraints. Without independent verification of their reported metrics (57,735 families, $163M in loans), consumers should treat these figures as organizational claims rather than independently audited facts.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors with bilingual capability (English/Spanish)
Free homebuyer education classes for Tampa and Orange County residents
Comprehensive lifecycle approach: pre-purchase, purchase, post-purchase, and foreclosure prevention support
Connections to layered down payment assistance up to $110,000 and network of experienced local realtors
HUD-approved certificates valid for 2 years, accepted by lenders and DPA programs
Post-purchase education covering uncommon topics like estate planning and scam avoidance
One-on-one personalized counseling available via phone, Zoom, and in-person
cons: Services appear limited to Tampa Bay/central Florida region; availability outside this area unclear
Rental support explicitly subject to funding availability, suggesting potential gaps in service access
Website does not provide transparent information about wait times, counselor availability, or response times
No independent audit or verification of reported impact metrics (57,735 families served, $163M in loans)
Limited detail on specific loan products offered through partnerships or how down payment assistance is accessed
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Tampa Bay/central Florida region seeking free HUD-certified education and personalized counseling
Current homeowners facing foreclosure or financial hardship seeking loss mitigation options
Renters in central Florida aspiring to transition to homeownership while maintaining housing stability
Spanish-speaking households needing bilingual housing and financial counseling
diagnosis: HEA is best for first-time homebuyers, renters, and struggling homeowners in central Florida seeking free, HUD-certified counseling and education. The main caveat is geographic limitation—services appear concentrated in the Tampa Bay area—and lack of transparent information about service availability, wait times, and independent verification of impact claims.
services: HUD-approved first-time homebuyer education (HomeTRACK) — self-paced online courses
Live virtual and in-person HUD-certified instructor-led homebuyer education classes
One-on-one HUD-certified housing counseling (phone, Zoom, in-person)
Bilingual budgeting and comprehensive financial assessment counseling
Credit review and improvement strategy guidance
Mortgage readiness and loan product options counseling
Layered down payment assistance navigation (up to $110,000)
Referrals to network of local realtors experienced with first-time homebuyer programs
Post-purchase homeownership education (budgeting, maintenance, insurance, taxes, estate planning)
Tenant rights, lease review, and rental readiness education
Eviction prevention and early intervention counseling
Foreclosure prevention and loan modification/forbearance option guidance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 9215 N Florida Ave Ste 104, Tampa, FL 33612
